focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A cabbie delivers a drenched, injured woman named Gabrielle to 221B Baker Street after finding her in the river; she has no memory and clutched the address. Holmes and Watson take her in.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Amnesiac woman delivered to 221B Baker Street
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gabrielle is brought to Holmes and Watson by a cabbie after being found drowning in the river; she has no memory and a bruise on her temple.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson begins to care for her, leading her upstairs to change out of wet clothes.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her identity and why she was in the river with the address are unknown.
evidence_quotes: GABRIELLE (looking up, trying to focus) I do not remember.
CABBIE From the river. I was driving down the Embankment, just below Westminster Bridge, and there she was in the water -- drowning.
He snatches the blanket off Gabrielle, revealing that her dress is clinging to her damply.

relation_updates: Watson begins providing care and comfort
Mrs. Hudson expresses concern
status_updates: Physical state: injured, wet, and amnesiac
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A vulnerable, amnesiac woman dependent on others for care.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes insists on hospitalizing Gabrielle for faster answers, but Watson refuses, prioritizing her safety and care amid her amnesia; he administers a sedative as Holmes pushes to uncover her identity urgently.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Amnesiac woman whose identity clues are uncovered through physical examination
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes and Watson determine she is Belgian from Brussels, named Gabrielle, married to Emile, and was deliberately attacked; Watson provides medical care and moderates Holmes's questioning
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Partial identity established but amnesia continues; remains under Watson's care at 221B Baker Street
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Reason for her presence in London and cause of the attack are still unknown
evidence_quotes: Vous etes Belge -- de Bruxelles!
Gabrielle - Emile
Watson is leading Gabrielle to a chair by the fireplace.
GABRIELLE I don't know.

relation_updates: Holmes and Watson actively investigate her background
Watson's protective stance is reinforced
status_updates: Identity clues established: Belgian, name Gabrielle, husband Emile
Head injury confirmed as deliberate attack
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Vulnerable patient protected by Watson from Holmes's intense investigation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gabrielle Valladon, attacked and nearly killed, seeks Holmes' help to find her husband Emile, who vanished after joining a fake company; Holmes plans to trace replies to a blank letter sent to Emile's last known address.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Client who has come to Holmes for assistance in locating her missing husband
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Appears disoriented in Holmes' dressing gown, confirms her identity as Gabrielle Valladon, and identifies her husband Emile from a photo presented by Holmes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To find her missing husband Emile Valladon
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Conscious but disoriented, under Holmes' care as he investigates her case.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The whereabouts of Emile Valladon and the cause of her disoriented, injured state.
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle appears from Holmes' bedroom wearing Holmes' dressing gown. She is unsteady on her feet and somewhat dazed.

relation_updates: Establishes formal client relationship with Holmes and Watson
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A disoriented woman seeking help to find her missing husband.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes receives a letter from Mycroft summoning him and Watson to the Diogenes Club by 11 a.m.; Watson adjusts his watch to match Mycroft's precise timing.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Active participant in the shop break-in and surveillance
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Physically assists in entering the shop via skylight and hides to observe an arriving old woman
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Concealed in the shop, watching an old woman in a wheelchair tend to canaries
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Purpose of the shop and identity of the old woman
evidence_quotes: Watson now helps Gabrielle through the skylight, lowers her toward Holmes, who puts his arms around her waist, eases her to the floor.
GABRIELLE I don't know how I'm going to pay you for all this. The purse with my money is somewhere at the bottom of the Thames.
They flatten themselves with their backs to the wall, just as the door slides open -- concealing them from view.

relation_updates: Closer physical interaction with Holmes during entry
Integrated into investigative team
status_updates: Now inside the shop, actively investigating
Witnesses arrival of mysterious old woman in wheelchair
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Co-investigator actively involved in field work

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes remains evasive about his plans involving Gabrielle, while Watson assists her in finding a lost glove, revealing underlying tensions and hidden motives in the case.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Client agreeing to deceptive travel plan
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hears Holmes's plan to travel under aliases to Inverness instead of returning to Brussels, apologizes for her earlier agitation, and agrees to pack for the journey.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To travel to Inverness with Holmes and Watson under false identities to continue searching for her husband.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Packing her belongings and preparing to depart from Euston Station as Mrs. Ashdown.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The fate of her husband and the true nature of the case.
evidence_quotes: GABRIELLE (moving toward Holmes) Thank you. I'm sorry for what I said. (kisses him on cheek)
She hurries off toward Watson's room.

relation_updates: Expresses gratitude and trust to Holmes with a kiss on the cheek
status_updates: Agrees to pack and prepare for travel under aliases as Mr. and Mrs. Ashdown
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Trusting client who accepts a deceptive cover identity.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes reveals the four midgets are missing acrobat brothers, prompting concern from Gabrielle and raising questions about a third grave's occupant.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother investigating son's disappearance at cemetery with Holmes and Watson
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Expresses relief after grave-digger states burials are of a father and two sons, unrelated to her son Emile
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Continue search for Emile with temporary emotional relief
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Relieved that Emile is not among the burials, still investigating
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Emile's location and safety remain unknown

relation_updates: Cooperative investigation with Holmes and Watson
status_updates: Emotional relief from immediate maternal fear
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Relieved mother temporarily reassured about her son's safety

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes examines Valladon's body in the coffin, noting his green wedding ring and three bleached white canaries at his feet, puzzling over their significance as Gabrielle remains unconscious.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Widow confronted with husband's corpse
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Screams and faints upon seeing her husband's corpse in the coffin
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Unconscious, caught by Watson
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Significance of green wedding ring and bleached white canaries in coffin
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle lets out a piercing scream
collapses in a faint
Watson catches her.

status_updates: Becomes unconscious after seeing husband's corpse
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Widow who faints at sight of husband's body

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes reassures Gabrielle, urges focus, and resumes inquiry as Watson interrupts with news of Mr. Ashdown's arrival.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Widow providing her wedding ring for Holmes's comparison and managing her emotional response.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes compares Gabrielle's ring with her husband's, notes a color difference, deduces the cause of death was not drowning, and instructs her to stop crying to maintain their cover as a holidaying couple.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gabrielle has ceased crying and attempted a tentative smile after Holmes's directive to control her emotions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The specific cause of Valladon's death is still unknown.
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle tearfully removes her ring, hands it to Holmes.
Gabrielle sinks down on the edge of one of the beds.
GABRIELLE (a final sniffle) I'll try.
She manages a tentative smile.

relation_updates: Holmes directs her to stop crying and maintain cover
Gabrielle complies and tries to smile
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A grieving widow attempting to regain composure under Holmes's guidance.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes realizes Ilse von Hoffmanstal was used by German agents to manipulate him into finding the air pump and now expects a royal visitor from Balmoral.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: German agent posing as Gabrielle Valladon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mycroft tells Holmes that Gabrielle Valladon is Ilse von Hoffmanstal, a German agent, and the real Madame Valladon is dead.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes has been informed that his client is an impostor working for the Imperial German Government.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Holmes will act on this knowledge about Ilse von Hoffmanstal.
evidence_quotes: MYCROFT Madame Valladon is dead.

relation_updates: Holmes learns she is an impostor and German agent
status_updates: Identity exposed as Ilse von Hoffmanstal
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Impersonator of a deceased woman, operating as a German intelligence agent.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes uses Gabrielle's parasol to send Morse code signaling the submersible's location, while she refuses to cooperate, and von Tirpitz's group observes from afar.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Exposed German agent refusing to cooperate with Holmes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes confronts her by addressing her as Fraulein Hoffmanstal, and she refuses to assist in signaling the submersible's location.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Exposed as Fraulein Hoffmanstal and non-cooperative, with Holmes taking control to send the signal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her fate as a captured agent with her cover blown.
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle makes no move to take the parasol
GABRIELLE I don't know. My German isn't that good.

relation_updates: Holmes openly calls her Fraulein Hoffmanstal
She refuses to cooperate with Holmes
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant captive refusing to cooperate after exposure.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mycroft and Gabrielle leave the hotel in a carriage with her suitcase; she does not look back as they drive away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Traveling with Mycroft Holmes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Leaves hotel in a carriage with Mycroft Holmes.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In the carriage with Mycroft, driving away from the hotel.
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle emerges, carrying her parasol, and accompanied by Mycroft.
He helps her into the carriage,
Gabrielle does not look back toward the hotel.

relation_updates: Begins journey accompanied by Mycroft Holmes
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Accompanied by Mycroft Holmes in a carriage.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gabrielle rides in a moving carriage away from the hotel, signaling with her parasol, which opens and closes repeatedly, unnoticed by Mycroft.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Signaling with parasol in carriage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Continues signaling with parasol while in carriage with Mycroft
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In moving carriage, signaling with parasol
evidence_quotes: Gabrielle has her parasol over her shoulder, and it opens and closes, opens and closes.
She continues to signal with the parasol, unnoticed by Mycroft.

relation_updates: Maintains covert action while with Mycroft
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Covert agent signaling unnoticed by companion

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Gabrielle Valladon is introduced as an amnesiac victim rescued from the river, who becomes a client of Holmes seeking her missing husband. She actively participates in the investigation, which leads to the discovery of her husband's body and a complex plot involving a mechanical monster. The investigation culminates in the revelation that she is actually Ilse von Hoffmanstal, a German agent who manipulated Holmes. After her exposure, she is taken away by Mycroft while covertly signaling.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes reprimands Mrs. Hudson for removing dust from his desk, explaining it is vital to his filing system for dating documents, then identifies a dust layer as March 1883.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes corrects Watson's exaggerated portrayals in his published stories.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes lists specific inaccuracies in Watson's account, including his height, costume, violin skills, attitude toward women, and cocaine use.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes and Watson are in tension over the discrepancies between Holmes' true self and his public image, with Watson disapproving of Holmes' cocaine habit.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The gap between Holmes' actual persona and the romanticized version in Watson's narratives.
evidence_quotes: Oh, come now, Watson, you must admit that you have a tendency to over-romanticize.
You have described me as six-foot-four, whereas I am barely six-foot-one.
You have painted me as a hopeless dope addict -- just because I occasionally take a five per cent solution of cocaine.
Five per cent. Don't you think I'm aware you've been diluting it behind my back?

relation_updates: Tension with Watson over public portrayal
Watson disapproves of Holmes' cocaine use
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes asserts his self-awareness and challenges Watson's embellishments.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes, restless and frustrated by inactivity, turns to cocaine use despite Watson's disapproval, highlighting his mental turmoil and dependency, while Watson observes helplessly as Holmes administers the drug.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes injects cocaine due to frustration from inactivity.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes, feeling suffocated by lack of activity, prepares and administers cocaine despite Watson's disapproval.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes is drawing cocaine into a hypodermic syringe.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Holmes' need for stimulation and potential dependency on cocaine.
evidence_quotes: HOLMES Not from lack of air -- from lack of activity.
He has removed the stopper from the cocaine bottle, and inserting the hypodermic needle into it, starts to draw up the liquid.

relation_updates: Watson disapproves of drug use
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows Holmes' tendency to use drugs when bored and his disregard for Watson's concerns.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes tricks Watson by destroying fake narcotics, hides real ones in his violin case, and expresses affection, reaffirming their bond despite his deceptive methods.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes tricks Watson into staying by pretending to destroy his narcotics.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes shoots substitute narcotics bottles, hides the real ones in his violin case, and expresses affection to Watson, leading Watson to decide to stay.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson decides to stay and unpack his belongings.
evidence_quotes: Holmes fires, smashing the second bottle.
he opens the violin case. Neatly stashed away around the edges are the three original bottles of narcotics.
I'm very fond of you, Watson.
while you unpack my things.

relation_updates: Reaffirms bond with Watson
Watson stays
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Demonstrates Holmes' deceptive methods to maintain his relationship with Watson, while showing genuine affection.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rogozhin spreads a false rumor about Watson and Holmes, causing the dancers to shun Watson, who realizes the misunderstanding too late and confronts Rogozhin.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes declines Petrova's proposal by disclosing his romantic relationship with Watson.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes refuses to father Petrova's child, revealing his involvement with Watson.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes remains in the dressing room after the disclosure.
evidence_quotes: HOLMES A bachelor -- living with another bachelor -- for the last five years. Five very happy years.
HOLMES The point is that Tschaikowski is not an isolated case.
ROGOZHIN You mean, you and Dr. Watson -- ? (Holmes nods) He is your glass of tea? HOLMES If you want to be picturesque about it.

relation_updates: Discloses relationship with Watson
Declines Petrova's offer
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows commitment to Watson through direct disclosure of their relationship.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A cabbie delivers a drenched, injured woman named Gabrielle to 221B Baker Street after finding her in the river; she has no memory and clutched the address. Holmes and Watson take her in.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes is confronted with an amnesiac woman delivered to his door.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: A cabbie brings an injured, amnesiac woman to 221B Baker Street, and Holmes questions why she was brought there.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes agrees to take in Gabrielle, and Watson begins caring for her.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gabrielle's identity and why she had the address 221B Baker Street.
evidence_quotes: HOLMES (from the upper landing) Why did you bring her here?
HOLMES Watson, you'd better accept delivery.
GABRIELLE (looking up, trying to focus) I do not remember.

relation_updates: Takes on Gabrielle as a client
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes shows curiosity and practicality in responding to an unexpected visitor.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gabrielle Valladon, attacked and nearly killed, seeks Holmes' help to find her husband Emile, who vanished after joining a fake company; Holmes plans to trace replies to a blank letter sent to Emile's last known address.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes devises a plan to trace Emile Valladon by sending a blank letter to his last known address.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After hearing Gabrielle Valladon's account, Holmes deduces her husband was a copper miner and instructs her to send a blank letter to trace him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To identify who collects mail from the empty shop on Ashdown Street.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes has instructed Gabrielle to send a blank letter to Emile's address, setting a trap to observe who picks it up.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The identity of Gabrielle's attacker and the purpose of the fake company Jonah Limited.
evidence_quotes: Your wedding ring -- it's made of copper.
HOLMES Madame Valladon, I want you to send one more letter to your husband.
HOLMES Nothing.

relation_updates: Client relationship with Gabrielle
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows deductive reasoning from a copper ring and strategic use of a blank letter as a trap.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mycroft asserts his authority over Sherlock, citing government orders to drop the case, and subtly mocks him by returning his cane as they depart.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mycroft orders Holmes to stop investigating an engineer due to national security.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mycroft explicitly tells Holmes to drop the case, citing government handling of the matter.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes receives a direct order from Mycroft to abandon his investigation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Holmes will comply with Mycroft's order to cease the case.
evidence_quotes: My suggestion is that you pursue it no further.
Because it involves the national security. We are handling this matter ourselves.

relation_updates: Conflict with Mycroft over investigation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes' investigative independence is challenged by Mycroft's official authority.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes ignores Watson's warning about defying Mycroft and the government; Gabrielle meets them at their flat, asking for news.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes does not respond to Watson's question about defying Mycroft's orders.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes ignores Watson's warning regarding disobedience to the government, as shown by his lack of reaction.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To continue the investigation despite prohibition.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: At 221B Baker Street with Gabrielle inquiring about news.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to proceed with the investigation without official sanction.
evidence_quotes: No reaction from Holmes.

relation_updates: Strain with Watson over defiance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Demonstrates Holmes' independence through non-engagement with warnings.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes shares his distrust of women, citing his fiancée's death from influenza just before their wedding as proof of their unreliability, and ends the conversation with Gabrielle.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes recounts his fiancée's death to Gabrielle to justify his distrust of women.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes discloses that his fiancée died of influenza twenty-four hours before their wedding, citing it as evidence that women are unreliable.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes ends the conversation by saying good night and pulling the curtain across his berth.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Holmes's distrust of women is reaffirmed and remains unaddressed.
evidence_quotes: I am not a whole-hearted admirer of womankind.
It just proves my contention that women are unreliable and not to be trusted.

relation_updates: Reveals personal history to Gabrielle
Affirms distrust of women
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes expresses a cynical view of women based on personal loss and negative experiences.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes realizes Ilse von Hoffmanstal was used by German agents to manipulate him into finding the air pump and now expects a royal visitor from Balmoral.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes is informed by Mycroft that he was manipulated by German agents to find an air pump invention.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mycroft reveals Holmes' client was the German government, Ilse von Hoffmanstal is a German agent, and the real Madame Valladon is dead.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes has been shown evidence of the deception and Ilse von Hoffmanstal's true identity.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Holmes will respond to the German plot and Ilse's role.
evidence_quotes: You, my dear brother, have been working for the Wilhelmstrasse.
Madame Valladon is dead.
Ilse von Hoffmanstal, one of their most skillful agents.
Holmes lowers the photograph.

relation_updates: Ilse von Hoffmanstal identified as German agent
Client was German government
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes realizes his deductions were exploited by foreign agents.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes reveals he wasn't fooled by Gabrielle from the start; she admits her failure in the mission, expressing regret, while Holmes downplays his own infallibility before Mycroft arrives to take her into custody.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Holmes reveals the trap for German agents and arranges for Gabrielle's arrest.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holmes tells Gabrielle he knew her identity as von Hoffmanstal and describes a plan to let German agents steal a submersible, setting them up for capture.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have German agents take the baited submersible and be intercepted at sea.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gabrielle is packing for custody by Mycroft; the submersible trap is prepared.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the German agents will take the submersible and be successfully captured.
evidence_quotes: We're going to let them have the submersible. They will find it with its engines running, all set to go.
I would suggest you get your things together. Mycroft will be here to take you into custody.
I never had you fooled for a moment, did I? You knew right from the beginning -- when the cabbie brought me to Baker Street.
Close enough.

relation_updates: Gabrielle revealed as Ilse von Hoffmanstal
Confirms partnership with Watson
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Holmes calmly explains his knowledge and sets a practical trap, demonstrating his methodical approach.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes's melancholic violin music continues, underscoring his emotional state and unavailability for the Whitechapel case as the scene fades out.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: After reading Mycroft's letter about Ilse's execution, Holmes withdraws and uses cocaine.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mycroft's letter informs Holmes of Ilse von Hoffmanstal's execution, leading him to retrieve cocaine and isolate himself.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Isolate himself in his bedroom with cocaine.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes is in his bedroom with cocaine, and Watson handles Inspector Lestrade.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Grief over Ilse's death and cocaine addiction.
evidence_quotes: HOLMES (holding up envelope) H'mmm. A letter from the Diogenes Club.
Slowly he puts down the letter, rises, crosses to the window, stands there staring out into the wintry street.
HOLMES (quietly, without turning) Where is it, Watson?
He sets the bag down on the desk, opens it, takes out a bottle of cocaine.

relation_updates: Watson handles Lestrade on Holmes' behalf
Mycroft delivers bad news
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows Holmes' emotional response and reliance on cocaine.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sherlock Holmes begins by confronting his public image and cocaine dependency, straining his relationship with Watson. He takes on a case involving Gabrielle Valladon, uncovering espionage and manipulation by German agents, which leads to royal recognition and a covert mission. After resolving the case, he receives news of a personal loss, relapses into cocaine use, and withdraws from active work.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A sealed manuscript from Dr. Watson reveals unpublished cases of Sherlock Holmes, including a 1887 Yorkshire murder, withheld for their sensitive and scandalous nature.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Watson, the doctor who recorded Holmes' cases and kept his mementos in a sealed box.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bank guards open Watson's sealed dispatch box, finding a manuscript where he explains withholding scandalous cases for discretion.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The manuscript is uncovered, and Watson's voice begins narrating a withheld case from 1887.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What specific scandalous cases are included in the manuscript?
evidence_quotes: The guards find a battered tin dispatch box with the name JOHN H. WATSON, M.D., painted on it.
But there were other adventures which, for reasons of discretion, I have decided to withhold from the public until this much later date.
They involve matters of a delicate and sometimes scandalous nature, as will shortly become apparent.

relation_updates: Reaffirms long association with Holmes through mementos
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson as the chronicler who withholds sensitive stories to protect privacy.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes reprimands Mrs. Hudson for removing dust from his desk, explaining it is vital to his filing system for dating documents, then identifies a dust layer as March 1883.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Friend, doctor, and chronicler of Holmes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson is criticized by Holmes for exaggerating details in his Strand Magazine story and admits to diluting Holmes' cocaine solution out of medical concern.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes and Watson are in open disagreement over Watson's writing and Holmes' drug use, with Watson defending his interventions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing conflict regarding Watson's narrative embellishments and Holmes' cocaine habit.
evidence_quotes: Oh, come now, Watson, you must admit that you have a tendency to over-romanticize.
Don't you think I'm aware you've been diluting it behind my back?
As a doctor -- and as your friend -- I strongly disapprove of this insidious habit of yours.

relation_updates: Holmes critiques Watson's writing
Watson reveals protective intervention in Holmes' drug use
status_updates: Watson is a doctor who disapproves of Holmes' cocaine habit
auxiliary.persona_anchor: The medically responsible friend who intervenes in Holmes' health matters.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Watson leaves Holmes, providing rent and a final dose of drugs, while Holmes is left alone, pacing and staring at the narcotics before turning to his chemistry table.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: departing housemate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson announces he is moving out of Baker Street, states his intention to resume medical practice, and gives Holmes a fresh hypodermic needle and narcotics as a farewell.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson has left the living room after declaring his move and providing drugs to Holmes.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Holmes will misuse the narcotics Watson left behind.
evidence_quotes: WATSON Mrs. Hudson, I want you to pack my bags.
WATSON And beyond. I'm moving out.
WATSON I don't know yet. But I intend to resume my practice. I am, after all, a doctor.

relation_updates: Watson ends his cohabitation with Holmes
Watson severs his role as Holmes's chronicler and companion
status_updates: Watson becomes a former housemate of Holmes
Watson is now a doctor planning to resume practice
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Doctor asserting his professional identity by resuming practice, while performing a final act of care for Holmes.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes tricks Watson by destroying fake narcotics, hides real ones in his violin case, and expresses affection, reaffirming their bond despite his deceptive methods.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Watson decides to continue living with Holmes after Holmes's affectionate reassurance.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson chooses to stay with Holmes, responding positively to Holmes's expression of fondness and instructing Mrs. Hudson to unpack his belongings.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson is cleaning up the broken glass and has told Mrs. Hudson to unpack his things, confirming his decision to stay.
evidence_quotes: while you unpack my things.
WATSON You've made me very happy.
WATSON I know that. But one likes to hear these things occasionally.

relation_updates: Watson-Holmes bond reaffirmed
Watson accepts Holmes's affection and deception
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson, who prioritizes his bond with Holmes over other options.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rogozhin spreads a false rumor about Watson and Holmes, causing the dancers to shun Watson, who realizes the misunderstanding too late and confronts Rogozhin.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: target of Rogozhin's false rumor about a homosexual relationship with Holmes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rogozhin whispers to dancers that Watson and Holmes are lovers, causing female dancers to shun Watson and male dancers to join him, leading to Watson's social isolation and distress.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson is bewildered and socially ostracized after learning of the rumor from Rogozhin.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Watson has confronted Rogozhin but must deal with the false rumor's impact on the ballet troupe.
evidence_quotes: He is dancing with a dozen of the ballerinas to madly accelerating balalaika music.
It gradually dawns on Watson that there is something wrong with this state of affairs.
Watson grabs the glass away from him, downs it with a gulp.

relation_updates: Rogozhin becomes actively hostile toward Watson
The ballet troupe's perception of Watson shifts from celebratory companion to ostracized outsider
status_updates: Watson's social status changes from popular guest to isolated figure
Watson's emotional state shifts from joyful intoxication to bewildered distress
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson as the oblivious party-goer whose reputation is tarnished by malicious gossip.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Watson hesitates and follows Holmes, who has dismissed his concerns, as the scene transitions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Watson confronts Holmes about the smoking machine deception and shifts to planning legal action against rumors.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson discovers Holmes's deception, expresses anger and fear for his reputation, then decides to confront rumors together with Holmes.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson and Holmes agree to face rumors together, but Holmes's silence on vouching from women leaves a concern.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Holmes's silence when asked if he can get women to vouch for him.
evidence_quotes: Watson, grim and breathless, stands in the open doorway.
WATSON There you are, you wretch! You rotter! You blackguard! Of all the vile, unspeakable fabrications.
WATSON (a change in attitude; defiant) The whole thing is ridiculous. We have nothing to hide.
WATSON Let somebody start a rumor -- just one ugly word -- and we'll sue them for slander.

relation_updates: Watson reaffirms partnership with Holmes despite anger
Watson and Holmes agree to face rumors together
status_updates: Watson's reputation is threatened by rumors
Watson decides to take a defiant stance against slander
auxiliary.persona_anchor: The publicly slandered man who chooses defiant solidarity over separation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A cabbie delivers a drenched, injured woman named Gabrielle to 221B Baker Street after finding her in the river; she has no memory and clutched the address. Holmes and Watson take her in.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Watson becomes an active caregiver by taking in Gabrielle.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson pays the cabbie and comforts Gabrielle, leading her upstairs.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson is escorting Gabrielle to get out of her wet clothes.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gabrielle's identity and how she obtained the address are unknown.
evidence_quotes: he starts down the stairs
Watson fishes some coins out of his pocket, hands them to the cabbie.

relation_updates: Establishes caring relationship with Gabrielle
auxiliary.persona_anchor: The instinctive caregiver who prioritizes immediate human need.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes insists on hospitalizing Gabrielle for faster answers, but Watson refuses, prioritizing her safety and care amid her amnesia; he administers a sedative as Holmes pushes to uncover her identity urgently.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: protector and medical authority
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson stops Holmes from questioning Gabrielle, sedates her with a sleeping potion, and refuses to hospitalize her, taking personal responsibility for her care in the flat.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Protect Gabrielle from further harm and provide medical care.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gabrielle sedated and placed in Watson's bed under his protection, with Watson sleeping on the couch.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gabrielle's amnesia and unknown identity.
evidence_quotes: That's enough, Holmes. I will not permit you to question her in this condition.
Under no circumstances.
I'd better mix her a sleeping potion.
He gets a packet of white powder out of his medical bag, and during the following, stirs a spoonful into her tea-cup.

relation_updates: Establishes protective stance toward Gabrielle
Asserts authority over Holmes regarding patient care
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A doctor who prioritizes patient protection over investigation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes remains evasive about his plans involving Gabrielle, while Watson assists her in finding a lost glove, revealing underlying tensions and hidden motives in the case.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Assigned as 'valet John' for the Inverness trip
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson is told he will act as valet in Holmes's plan to travel to Inverness under aliases.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To travel to Inverness as part of the disguised group with Holmes and Gabrielle.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson is set to assume the valet role but questions Holmes about the plan.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Watson does not know Holmes's true intentions or the full scope of the plan.
evidence_quotes: WATSON (acidly) Maybe I should do it, since I'm the valet.
WATSON Holmes, what exactly are you up to?

relation_updates: Assigned valet role in joint operation with Holmes
Cooperative interaction with Gabrielle (glove retrieval)
status_updates: Becomes 'valet John' for the Inverness mission
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson cooperates with the assigned role but openly questions Holmes's secrecy.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Watson rushes in, claiming to have seen the monster in the lake from the attic, and frantically searches for the telescope to confirm his sighting.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: assistant and observer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson bursts in, reports seeing the monster in the lake from the attic, and urgently searches for the telescope to verify.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to confirm the monster sighting with the telescope
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Watson is on the balcony with the telescope, attempting to look for the monster.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Watson's sighting of the monster is accurate.
evidence_quotes: Watson bursts in breathlessly.
Holmes! I saw it! I saw it from the attic! It's out there in the lake!
The monster!
Watson fishes a small telescope out of Holmes' suitcase, rushes through the open French window to the balcony.

status_updates: has made a potential sighting of the monster
is on the balcony with telescope
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson is excited and urgent in his report, contrasting with Holmes's questioning.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holmes's melancholic violin music continues, underscoring his emotional state and unavailability for the Whitechapel case as the scene fades out.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Caretaker and gatekeeper for Holmes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watson reads Mycroft's letter informing him of Ilse von Hoffmanstal's execution, sees Holmes take cocaine and retreat to his bedroom, and tells Inspector Lestrade that Holmes is unavailable for the Whitechapel case.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To handle external requests and support Holmes during his withdrawal.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holmes is in his bedroom with cocaine, and Watson has declined Lestrade's request for help on the Whitechapel murders.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Whitechapel murders case is introduced but not pursued by Holmes.
evidence_quotes: WATSON Holmes -- I'm terribly sorry about this.
HOLMES (quietly, without turning) Where is it, Watson? WATSON (after a beat) In the files. May to July, 1885.
Watson approaches the door, opens it to reveal INSPECTOR LESTRADE
WATSON (a glance toward the bedroom) I'm sorry, Lestrade. But at the moment, Holmes is working on another problem.

relation_updates: Watson expresses sympathy to Holmes over Ilse's death
Watson acts as gatekeeper, turning down Lestrade's request for Holmes
status_updates: Watson becomes aware of Ilse von Hoffmanstal's execution
Watson observes Holmes's retreat into cocaine use
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watson manages visitors and makes decisions on Holmes's behalf when Holmes is incapacitated.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Watson begins as Holmes's chronicler and housemate, navigating tension over his writing and Holmes's drug use. After a failed attempt to leave Baker Street, he becomes embroiled in social scandal and then a new case involving an amnesiac woman, Gabrielle. He assumes protective, medical, and operational roles, ultimately becoming Holmes's caretaker when Holmes retreats into grief and addiction.
